title: Earleaf: an offline audiobook app built out of frustration
author: Ellsworth Toohey
summary: A developer switched from iPhone to Android after 15 years and missed his audiobook app so much that he built one. Earleaf is $4.99, with no ads, no subscriptions, and no tracking. It plays locally stored audiobooks. The killer feature is Page Sync — photograph a page from your physical book and the app finds that exact spot in the audio within about two seconds. —
